projects.rebuild.overview.title: site.hopen
Overview
site.hclose
projects.rebuild.overview.1: Once the decision had been made to restore the old machine, it was completely stripped down and the old paint removed.
projects.rebuild.overview.2: The casting itself was crude and little effort had been expended in finishing it properly. Rather than grinding and
dressing the surfaces, some kind of body filler had simply been plastered over the casting to create the illusion
of a smooth finish beneath the paint.
projects.rebuild.overview.3: All components were cleaned and repainted, and new bearings installed. As fortune would have it, my Harley bearing-puller
turned out to be exactly the right size for the job.
projects.rebuild.overview.4: My presumption is that the machine was originally intended to be driven from an overhead line shaft, possibly with a
countershaft arrangement mounted on the sliding platform behind the headstock. Surprisingly, the original drive pulley
was found amongst the assorted parts acquired with the machine.
projects.rebuild.overview.5: The pulley contained a home-made aluminium bush. A single-phase 2 h.p. motor was purchased, however the motor shaft
utilised a 1/4" key. A matching keyway was cut by hand using a sharpened and honed piece of 1/4" square tool steel.
projects.rebuild.overview.6: The motor was ultimately mounted onto the sliding platform. Holes were drilled and tapped to accommodate the motor's
